primary_sources.title: CloudEvents Specification
relevant_rules: Every event has required id, source, specversion, and type context attributes.
The pair source plus id identifies duplicates.
An event is a record expressing an occurrence and context; it is not proof that later work completed.

finding: Peer coordination needs an event identity envelope plus a project-specific state contract. A trigger request, delivery attempt, acceptance, completion, and failure are distinct occurrences and must not be represented as one success claim.
design_implication: Use CloudEvents-compatible identity fields for interoperability, while requiring state-specific evidence. Completion is accepted only when a peer-produced output reference and full peer commit SHA are present.

performed: GitHub accepted both source files on the default branch.
The committed test defines five deterministic cases covering valid attempt, state mismatch, unsupported completion, evidence-backed completion, and failed-without-reason rejection.
not_performed: No GitHub Actions or local Node execution result was available in this run.
No peer completion receipt exists yet.

falsification_route: Run node --test tools/frontier-research/peer-trigger-receipt.test.mjs in a clean checkout.
Attempt to validate a completed receipt lacking peer_output_ref or a 40-character peer_commit; acceptance falsifies the contract.
Attempt to reuse the same source plus id for a distinct occurrence; downstream deduplication must flag it.
limits: A structurally valid receipt does not prove the referenced evidence is truthful.
CloudEvents identity does not provide authentication or cryptographic integrity.
Peer completion must still be independently checked against repository and runtime evidence.
